{{Short description|Italian film festival in 2022}} {{EngvarB|date=July 2022}} {{Use dmy dates|date=June 2022}} {{Infobox Film festival | name = 79th Venice International Film Festival | image = 79th Venice Film Festival poster.jpg | caption = Official festival poster by [[Lorenzo Mattotti]] | opening = ''[[White Noise (2022 film)|White Noise]]'' | closing = ''[[The Hanging Sun]]'' | location = [[Venice]], [[Italy]] | founded = 1932 | awards = [[Golden Lion]]:
''[[All the Beauty and the Bloodshed]]'' | host = [[Rocío Muñoz Morales]] | date = 31 August – 10 September 2022 | website = {{URL|https://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/2022}} | chronology = Venice Film Festival | preceded_by = [[78th Venice International Film Festival|78th]] | followed_by =[[80th Venice International Film Festival|80th]] |artistic_director=[[Alberto Barbera]]}} The '''79th annual [[Venice Film Festival|Venice International Film Festival]]''' was held from 31 August to 10 September 2022, at [[Venice Lido]] in [[Italy]].{{cite web|url=https://www.labiennale.org/it/news/roc%C3%ADo-mu%C3%B1oz-morales-condurr%C3%A0-le-serate-di-apertura-e-chiusura-di-venezia-79|title=Rocío Munos Morales to host the opening and closing nights of Venezia 79|date=6 May 2022|access-date=28 June 2022|archive-date=11 May 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220511202404/https://www.labiennale.org/it/news/roc%C3%ADo-mu%C3%B1oz-morales-condurr%C3%A0-le-serate-di-apertura-e-chiusura-di-venezia-79|url-status=live}}{{cite web|url=https://www.screendaily.com/news/venice-film-festival-unveils-2022-line-up-follow-live/5172911.article|title=Venice film festival unveils 2022 line-up - follow live|publisher=ScreenDaily|access-date=26 July 2022|archive-date=26 July 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220726090005/https://www.screendaily.com/news/venice-film-festival-unveils-2022-line-up-follow-live/5172911.article|url-status=live}}{{cite web|url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/venice-2022-film-festival-lineup-1235185580/|title=Venice Film Festival Unveils Competition Lineup (Updating Live)|date=26 July 2022 |publisher=The Hollywood Reporter|access-date=26 July 2022|archive-date=26 July 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220726095900/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/venice-2022-film-festival-lineup-1235185580/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=social|url-status=live}} American actress [[Julianne Moore]] was the Jury President of the main competition. Spanish actress [[Rocío Muñoz Morales]] hosted the opening and closing nights of the festival.The [[Golden Lion]] was awarded to ''[[All the Beauty and the Bloodshed]]'' by [[Laura Poitras]] making it the second documentary, following [[Gianfranco Rosi (director)|Gianfranco Rosi]]'s ''[[Sacro GRA]]'' in 2013, to win the festival's top prize.{{cite web |date=10 September 2022 |title=Venice Film Festival Winners: Cate Blanchett, Colin Farrell Take Top Acting Prizes – Updating Live |url=https://deadline.com/2022/09/venice-film-festival-2022-winners-golden-lion-full-list-1235113993/ |access-date=10 September 2022 |work=Deadline Hollywood}} American filmmaker [[Paul Schrader]] and French actress [[Catherine Deneuve]] were awarded the Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement. The festival opened with ''[[White Noise (2022 film)|White Noise]]'' by [[Noah Baumbach]], and closed with ''[[The Hanging Sun]]'' by [[Francesco Carrozzini]]. == Juries == === Main competition (''Venezia 79'') === * [[Julianne Moore]], American actress and author - Jury President{{Cite web|url=https://variety.com/2022/film/global/julianne-moore-venice-jury-audrey-diwan-1235317544/|website=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]|first=Nick|last=Vivarelli|date=15 July 2022|title=Julianne Moore to Head Venice Jury Also Comprising Audrey Diwan, Leila Hatami, Kazuo Ishiguro|access-date=15 July 2022|archive-date=15 July 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220715141559/https://variety.com/2022/film/global/julianne-moore-venice-jury-audrey-diwan-1235317544/|url-status=live}} * [[Mariano Cohn]], Argentine director, screenwriter and producer * [[Leonardo Di Costanzo]], Italian director and screenwriter * [[Audrey Diwan]], French director and screenwriter * [[Leila Hatami]], Iranian actress * [[Kazuo Ishiguro]], British novelist and screenwriter * [[Rodrigo Sorogoyen]], Spanish director and screenwriter === Horizons (''Orizzonti'') === * [[Isabel Coixet]], Spanish filmmaker - Jury President{{Cite web |last=Vivarelli |first=Nick |date=20 July 2022 |title=Spanish Director Isabel Coixet to Head Venice Horizons Jury |url=https://variety.com/2022/film/global/isabel-coixet-venice-horizons-jury-1235320873/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220720154055/https://variety.com/2022/film/global/isabel-coixet-venice-horizons-jury-1235320873/ |archive-date=20 July 2022 |access-date=20 July 2022 |website=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]}} * [[Laura Bispuri]], Italian filmmaker * [[Antonio Campos (director)|Antonio Campos]], American filmmaker and producer * [[Sofia Djama]], Algerian filmmaker * {{ill|Édouard Waintrop|fr}}, French film critic === Luigi de Laurentis Award === * [[Michelangelo Frammartino]], Italian filmmaker - Jury President * [[Jan P.
